summary: PES-001 passes all 5 hard gates: 3 Active ERs with full coverage, all inputs confirmed non-Draft, all pattern findings cite specific artifact IDs and counts, §6 contains one improvement proposal with all required fields, proposal is specific and actionable.

warnings.description: Only 2 declared incidents across 3 services — root cause class frequency analysis is noted as insufficient for generalization. This is correctly flagged in §4 rather than overstated. However, the first-period vulnerability finding (2 of 3 services) is drawn from SLO miss data rather than incident data, which is valid but worth distinguishing clearly. The §4 analysis does make this distinction.

Market Timing assumptions (2 of 2 not tested) are noted in §5 as hard to test experimentally in short discovery cycles — this is accurate context but the PES does not propose any improvement to address this structural limitation. A future PES with more initiatives may warrant a proposal to the AR spec or EL prompt to explicitly classify untestable assumptions and handle them differently at DPRD handoff.

P-1 proposes a change to es-prompt.md Step 7. The example addition text in the proposal is illustrative but will require refinement during the amendment process to fit the prompt's existing instruction style. The proposal is specific enough to implement; the exact wording should be finalized by the kit maintainer.
